text: We will start directly abstract linear algebra, taking place in a vector space over any field. However you can find information about matrices in Section 10.4.1 which does not logically depend on this abstract theory. Mathlib actually deals with a more general version of linear algebra involving the word module, but for now we will pretend this is only an eccentric spelling habit.
html: We will start directly abstract linear algebra, taking place in a vector space over any field. However you can find information about matrices in Section 10.4.1 which does not logically depend on this abstract theory. Mathlib actually deals with a more general version of linear algebra involving the word module, but for now we will pretend this is only an eccentric spelling habit.
zh: 我们将直接从抽象线性代数开始，即任意域上的向量空间中的线性代数。不过，关于矩阵的内容可见Section 10.4.1，该节在逻辑上并不依赖这套抽象理论。实际上，Mathlib 处理的是更一般的线性代数，其中会出现“模”这一术语；但暂时我们可以把这看作一种略显特殊的命名习惯。

text: We explained in Chapter 8 why we need two separate typeclasses [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V] . The short version is the following. Mathematically we want to say that having a \(K\) -vector space structure implies having an additive commutative group structure. We could tell this to Lean. But then whenever Lean would need to find such a group structure on a type \(V\) , it would go hunting for vector space structures using a completely unspecified field \(K\) that cannot be inferred from \(V\) . This would be very bad for the type class synthesis system.
html: We explained in Chapter 8 why we need two separate typeclasses [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]. The short version is the following. Mathematically we want to say that having a \(K\)-vector space structure implies having an additive commutative group structure. We could tell this to Lean. But then whenever Lean would need to find such a group structure on a type \(V\), it would go hunting for vector space structures using a completely unspecified field \(K\) that cannot be inferred from \(V\). This would be very bad for the type class synthesis system.
zh: 我们在Chapter 8中解释过，为什么需要两个分开的类型类 [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]。简短地说，数学上我们想表达的是：具有 \(K\)-向量空间结构蕴含具有加法交换群结构。我们当然可以把这一点告诉 Lean。但这样一来，每当 Lean 需要在某个类型 \(V\) 上寻找这样的群结构时，它就会开始寻找相对于某个完全未指定的域 \(K\) 的向量空间结构，而这个域无法从 \(V\) 推断出来。这会严重损害类型类合成系统的工作。

text: The multiplication of a vector v by a scalar a is denoted by a • v . We list a couple of algebraic rules about the interaction of this operation with addition in the following examples. Of course simp or apply? would find those proofs. There is also a module tactic that solves goals following from the axioms of vector spaces and fields, in the same way the ring tactic is used in commutative rings or the group tactic is used in groups. But it is still useful to remember that scalar multiplication is abbreviated smul in lemma names.
html: The multiplication of a vector v by a scalar a is denoted by a • v. We list a couple of algebraic rules about the interaction of this operation with addition in the following examples. Of course simp or apply? would find those proofs. There is also a module tactic that solves goals following from the axioms of vector spaces and fields, in the same way the ring tactic is used in commutative rings or the group tactic is used in groups. But it is still useful to remember that scalar multiplication is abbreviated smul in lemma names.
zh: 向量 <cite>v</cite> 乘以标量 <cite>a</cite> 记作 <cite>a • v</cite>。下面的例子列出这一运算与加法相互作用的若干代数规则。当然，<cite>simp</cite> 或 <cite>apply?</cite> 都可以找到这些证明。还有一个 <cite>module</cite> 策略，它会解决由向量空间和域的公理推出的目标，正如在交换环中使用 <cite>ring</cite> 策略、在群中使用 <cite>group</cite> 策略一样。不过仍然有必要记住，在引理名称中，标量乘法通常缩写为 <cite>smul</cite>。

text: Next we need linear maps. Like group morphisms, linear maps in Mathlib are bundled maps, i.e. packages made of a map and proofs of its linearity properties. Those bundled maps are converted to ordinary functions when applied. See Chapter 8 for more information about this design.
html: Next we need linear maps. Like group morphisms, linear maps in Mathlib are bundled maps, i.e. packages made of a map and proofs of its linearity properties. Those bundled maps are converted to ordinary functions when applied. See Chapter 8 for more information about this design.
zh: 接下来需要线性映射。与群态射一样，Mathlib 中的线性映射是打包映射，也就是由一个映射及其线性性质的证明组成的对象。在应用这些打包映射时，它们会被转换为通常的函数。关于这种设计，参见Chapter 8。

text: The type of linear maps between two K -vector spaces V and W is denoted by V →ₗ[K] W . The subscript l stands for linear. At first it may feel odd to specify K in this notation. But this is crucial when several fields come into play. For instance real-linear maps from \(ℂ\) to \(ℂ\) are every map \(z ↦ az + b\bar{z}\) while only the maps \(z ↦ az\) are complex linear, and this difference is crucial in complex analysis.
html: The type of linear maps between two K-vector spaces V and W is denoted by V →ₗ[K] W. The subscript l stands for linear. At first it may feel odd to specify K in this notation. But this is crucial when several fields come into play. For instance real-linear maps from \(ℂ\) to \(ℂ\) are every map \(z ↦ az + b\bar{z}\) while only the maps \(z ↦ az\) are complex linear, and this difference is crucial in complex analysis.
zh: 两个 K-向量空间 V 与 W 之间的线性映射类型记作 V →ₗ[K] W。下标 l 表示 linear。初看之下，在这个记号中指定 K 或许有些奇怪；但当多个域同时出现时，这一点非常关键。例如，从 \(ℂ\) 到 \(ℂ\) 的实线性映射是所有形如 \(z ↦ az + b\bar{z}\) 的映射，而复线性映射只有形如 \(z ↦ az\) 的映射，这一区别在复分析中至关重要。

text: Note that V →ₗ[K] W itself carries interesting algebraic structures (this is part of the motivation for bundling those maps). It is a K -vector space so we can add linear maps and multiply them by scalars.
html: Note that V →ₗ[K] W itself carries interesting algebraic structures (this is part of the motivation for bundling those maps). It is a K-vector space so we can add linear maps and multiply them by scalars.
zh: 注意，V →ₗ[K] W 本身也带有有意义的代数结构（这也是将这些映射打包的动机之一）。它是一个 K-向量空间，因此可以对线性映射相加，也可以用标量乘以线性映射。

text: One downside of using bundled maps is that we cannot use ordinary function composition. We need to use LinearMap.comp or the notation ∘ₗ .
html: One downside of using bundled maps is that we cannot use ordinary function composition. We need to use LinearMap.comp or the notation ∘ₗ.
zh: 使用打包映射的一个代价是，不能直接使用通常的函数复合。我们需要使用 LinearMap.comp，或者使用记号 ∘ₗ。

text: There are two main ways to construct linear maps. First we can build the structure by providing the function and the linearity proof. As usual, this is facilitated by the structure code action: you can type example : V →ₗ[K] V := _ and use the code action “Generate a skeleton” attached to the underscore.
html: There are two main ways to construct linear maps. First we can build the structure by providing the function and the linearity proof. As usual, this is facilitated by the structure code action: you can type example : V →ₗ[K] V := _ and use the code action “Generate a skeleton” attached to the underscore.
zh: 构造线性映射主要有两种方式。第一种是直接构造该结构，给出函数以及线性性的证明。照常，结构代码操作可以简化这一步：你可以输入 example : V →ₗ[K] V := _，然后在下划线处使用“Generate a skeleton”代码操作生成骨架。

text: You may wonder why the proof fields of LinearMap have names ending with a prime. This is because they are defined before the coercion to functions is defined, hence they are phrased in terms of LinearMap.toFun . Then they are restated as LinearMap.map_add and LinearMap.map_smul in terms of the coercion to function. This is not yet the end of the story. One also wants a version of map_add that applies to any (bundled) map preserving addition, such as additive group morphisms, linear maps, continuous linear maps, K -algebra maps etc… This one is map_add (in the root namespace). The intermediate version, LinearMap.map_add is a bit redundant but allows to use dot notation, which can be nice sometimes. A similar story exists for map_smul , and the general framework is explained in Chapter 8 .
html: You may wonder why the proof fields of LinearMap have names ending with a prime. This is because they are defined before the coercion to functions is defined, hence they are phrased in terms of LinearMap.toFun. Then they are restated as LinearMap.map_add and LinearMap.map_smul in terms of the coercion to function. This is not yet the end of the story. One also wants a version of map_add that applies to any (bundled) map preserving addition, such as additive group morphisms, linear maps, continuous linear maps, K-algebra maps etc… This one is map_add (in the root namespace). The intermediate version, LinearMap.map_add is a bit redundant but allows to use dot notation, which can be nice sometimes. A similar story exists for map_smul, and the general framework is explained in Chapter 8.
zh: 你可能会疑惑，为什么 LinearMap 的证明字段名称以撇号结尾。这是因为它们定义时，到函数的强制转换尚未定义，所以这些字段要用 LinearMap.toFun 来表述。随后，它们又以关于到函数的强制转换的形式被重新陈述为 LinearMap.map_add 和 LinearMap.map_smul。事情还没有到此为止。我们还希望有一个适用于任何保持加法的（打包）映射的 map_add 版本，例如加法群态射、线性映射、连续线性映射、K-代数映射等。这个版本就是根命名空间中的 map_add。中间版本 LinearMap.map_add 有些冗余，但允许使用点记号，有时很方便。map_smul 也有类似情况；一般框架见Chapter 8。

text: One can also build linear maps from the ones that are already defined in Mathlib using various combinators. For instance the above example is already known as LinearMap.lsmul K V 3 . There are several reason why K and V are explicit arguments here. The most pressing one is that from a bare LinearMap.lsmul 3 there would be no way for Lean to infer V or even K . But also LinearMap.lsmul K V is an interesting object by itself: it has type K →ₗ[K] V →ₗ[K] V , meaning it is a K -linear map from K —seen as a vector space over itself— to the space of K -linear maps from V to V .
html: One can also build linear maps from the ones that are already defined in Mathlib using various combinators. For instance the above example is already known as LinearMap.lsmul K V 3. There are several reason why K and V are explicit arguments here. The most pressing one is that from a bare LinearMap.lsmul 3 there would be no way for Lean to infer V or even K. But also LinearMap.lsmul K V is an interesting object by itself: it has type K →ₗ[K] V →ₗ[K] V, meaning it is a K-linear map from K —seen as a vector space over itself— to the space of K-linear maps from V to V.
zh: 还可以借助各种组合子，从 Mathlib 中已经定义好的线性映射构造新的线性映射。例如，上面的例子已经作为 LinearMap.lsmul K V 3 存在。这里把 K 和 V 作为显式参数有若干原因。最直接的原因是，如果只有 LinearMap.lsmul 3，Lean 无法推断 V，甚至也无法推断 K。此外，LinearMap.lsmul K V 本身也是一个有意义的对象：它的类型是 K →ₗ[K] V →ₗ[K] V，意思是一个从 K（视为自身上的向量空间）到从 V 到 V 的 K-线性映射空间的 K-线性映射。

text: There is also a type LinearEquiv of linear isomorphisms denoted by V ≃ₗ[K] W . The inverse of f : V ≃ₗ[K] W is f.symm : W ≃ₗ[K] V , composition of f and g is f.trans g also denoted by f ≪≫ₗ g , and the identity isomorphism of V is LinearEquiv.refl K V . Elements of this type are automatically coerced to morphisms and functions when necessary.
html: There is also a type LinearEquiv of linear isomorphisms denoted by V ≃ₗ[K] W. The inverse of f : V ≃ₗ[K] W is f.symm : W ≃ₗ[K] V, composition of f and g is f.trans g also denoted by f ≪≫ₗ g, and the identity isomorphism of V is LinearEquiv.refl K V. Elements of this type are automatically coerced to morphisms and functions when necessary.
zh: 还有一个线性同构类型 LinearEquiv，记作 V ≃ₗ[K] W。若 f : V ≃ₗ[K] W，则其逆为 f.symm : W ≃ₗ[K] V；f 与 g 的复合是 f.trans g，也记作 f ≪≫ₗ g；V 的恒等同构是 LinearEquiv.refl K V。必要时，这个类型的元素会自动强制转换为态射和函数。

text: One can use LinearEquiv.ofBijective to build an isomorphism from a bijective morphism. Doing so makes the inverse function noncomputable.
html: One can use LinearEquiv.ofBijective to build an isomorphism from a bijective morphism. Doing so makes the inverse function noncomputable.
zh: 可以使用 LinearEquiv.ofBijective 从一个双射态射构造同构。这样做会使逆函数成为不可计算对象。

text: Note that in the above example, Lean uses the announced type to understand that .ofBijective refers to LinearEquiv.ofBijective (without needing to open any namespace).
html: Note that in the above example, Lean uses the announced type to understand that .ofBijective refers to LinearEquiv.ofBijective (without needing to open any namespace).
zh: 注意，在上面的例子中，Lean 利用已声明的类型来理解 .ofBijective 指的是 LinearEquiv.ofBijective，无需打开任何命名空间。

text: We can build new vector spaces out of old ones using direct sums and direct products. Let us start with two vectors spaces. In this case there is no difference between sum and product, and we can simply use the product type. In the following snippet of code we simply show how to get all the structure maps (inclusions and projections) as linear maps, as well as the universal properties constructing linear maps into products and out of sums (if you are not familiar with the category-theoretic distinction between sums and products, you can simply ignore the universal property vocabulary and focus on the types of the following examples).
html: We can build new vector spaces out of old ones using direct sums and direct products. Let us start with two vectors spaces. In this case there is no difference between sum and product, and we can simply use the product type. In the following snippet of code we simply show how to get all the structure maps (inclusions and projections) as linear maps, as well as the universal properties constructing linear maps into products and out of sums (if you are not familiar with the category-theoretic distinction between sums and products, you can simply ignore the universal property vocabulary and focus on the types of the following examples).
zh: 可以用直和与直积从已有向量空间构造新的向量空间。先考虑两个向量空间的情形。此时和与积没有区别，可以直接使用积类型。在下面的代码片段中，我们只是展示如何把所有结构映射（嵌入与投影）作为线性映射得到，以及如何使用泛性质来构造进入积和离开和的线性映射；如果你不熟悉范畴论中和与积的区别，可以忽略泛性质这一术语，只关注下面例子的类型。

text: Let us now turn to sums and products of arbitrary families of vector spaces. Here we will simply see how to define a family of vector spaces and access the universal properties of sums and products. Note that the direct sum notation is scoped to the DirectSum namespace, and that the universal property of direct sums requires decidable equality on the indexing type (this is somehow an implementation accident).
html: Let us now turn to sums and products of arbitrary families of vector spaces. Here we will simply see how to define a family of vector spaces and access the universal properties of sums and products. Note that the direct sum notation is scoped to the DirectSum namespace, and that the universal property of direct sums requires decidable equality on the indexing type (this is somehow an implementation accident).
zh: 现在转向任意族向量空间的和与积。这里我们只说明如何定义一个向量空间族，以及如何使用和与积的泛性质。注意，直和记号限定在 DirectSum 命名空间中，并且直和的泛性质要求指标类型具有可判定相等性（这多少是实现层面的偶然现象）。

text: Just as linear maps are bundled, a linear subspace of V is also a bundled structure consisting of a set in V , called the carrier of the subspace, with the relevant closure properties. Again the word module appears instead of vector space because of the more general context that Mathlib actually uses for linear algebra.
html: Just as linear maps are bundled, a linear subspace of V is also a bundled structure consisting of a set in V, called the carrier of the subspace, with the relevant closure properties. Again the word module appears instead of vector space because of the more general context that Mathlib actually uses for linear algebra.
zh: 正如线性映射是打包的，V 的线性子空间也是一个打包结构，由 V 中的一个集合（称为该子空间的载体）以及相关闭包性质组成。这里同样出现“模”而不是“向量空间”一词，是因为 Mathlib 的线性代数实际工作在更一般的语境中。

text: In the example above, it is important to understand that Submodule K V is the type of K -linear subspaces of V , rather than a predicate IsSubmodule U where U is an element of Set V . Submodule K V is endowed with a coercion to Set V and a membership predicate on V . See Section 8.3 for an explanation of how and why this is done.
html: In the example above, it is important to understand that Submodule K V is the type of K-linear subspaces of V, rather than a predicate IsSubmodule U where U is an element of Set V. Submodule K V is endowed with a coercion to Set V and a membership predicate on V. See Section 8.3 for an explanation of how and why this is done.
zh: 在上面的例子中，必须理解 Submodule K V 是 V 的 K-线性子空间的类型，而不是谓词 IsSubmodule U，其中 U 是 Set V 的元素。Submodule K V 带有到 Set V 的强制转换，并且在 V 上有成员谓词。关于这样做的方式与原因，参见Section 8.3。

text: Of course, two subspaces are the same if and only if they have the same elements. This fact is registered for use with the ext tactic, which can be used to prove two subspaces are equal in the same way it is used to prove that two sets are equal.
html: Of course, two subspaces are the same if and only if they have the same elements. This fact is registered for use with the ext tactic, which can be used to prove two subspaces are equal in the same way it is used to prove that two sets are equal.
zh: 当然，两个子空间相同当且仅当它们具有相同元素。这个事实已注册给 ext 策略使用；因此可以像证明两个集合相等一样，用该策略证明两个子空间相等。

text: To state and prove, for example, that ℝ is a ℝ -linear subspace of ℂ , what we really want is to construct a term of type Submodule ℝ ℂ whose projection to Set ℂ is ℝ , or, more precisely, the image of ℝ in ℂ .
html: To state and prove, for example, that ℝ is a ℝ-linear subspace of ℂ, what we really want is to construct a term of type Submodule ℝ ℂ whose projection to Set ℂ is ℝ, or, more precisely, the image of ℝ in ℂ.
zh: 例如，要陈述并证明 ℝ 是 ℂ 的一个 ℝ-线性子空间，我们真正想构造的是一个类型为 Submodule ℝ ℂ 的项，使其到 Set ℂ 的投影为 ℝ；更准确地说，是 ℝ 在 ℂ 中的像。

text: The prime at the end of proof fields in Submodule are analogous to the one in LinearMap . Those fields are stated in terms of the carrier field because they are defined before the MemberShip instance. They are then superseded by Submodule.add_mem , Submodule.zero_mem and Submodule.smul_mem that we saw above.
html: The prime at the end of proof fields in Submodule are analogous to the one in LinearMap. Those fields are stated in terms of the carrier field because they are defined before the MemberShip instance. They are then superseded by Submodule.add_mem, Submodule.zero_mem and Submodule.smul_mem that we saw above.
zh: Submodule 中证明字段末尾的撇号与 LinearMap 中的情形类似。这些字段用 carrier 字段来表述，因为它们定义时 MemberShip 实例尚未定义。随后，我们上面见过的 Submodule.add_mem、Submodule.zero_mem 和 Submodule.smul_mem 会取代这些字段。

text: As an exercise in manipulating subspaces and linear maps, you will define the pre-image of a subspace by a linear map (of course we will see below that Mathlib already knows about this). Remember that Set.mem_preimage can be used to rewrite a statement involving membership and preimage. This is the only lemma you will need in addition to the lemmas discussed above about LinearMap and Submodule .
html: As an exercise in manipulating subspaces and linear maps, you will define the pre-image of a subspace by a linear map (of course we will see below that Mathlib already knows about this). Remember that Set.mem_preimage can be used to rewrite a statement involving membership and preimage. This is the only lemma you will need in addition to the lemmas discussed above about LinearMap and Submodule.
zh: 作为一个操作子空间和线性映射的练习，你将定义线性映射下子空间的原像（当然，下面会看到 Mathlib 已经知道这一构造）。请记住，Set.mem_preimage 可以用来改写涉及成员关系和原像的命题。除了上面讨论过的关于 LinearMap 与 Submodule 的引理之外，这是唯一需要的引理。

text: Using type classes, Mathlib knows that a subspace of a vector space inherits a vector space structure.
html: Using type classes, Mathlib knows that a subspace of a vector space inherits a vector space structure.
zh: 借助类型类，Mathlib 知道向量空间的子空间继承向量空间结构。

text: This example is subtle. The object U is not a type, but Lean automatically coerces it to a type by interpreting it as a subtype of V . So the above example can be restated more explicitly as:
html: This example is subtle. The object U is not a type, but Lean automatically coerces it to a type by interpreting it as a subtype of V. So the above example can be restated more explicitly as:
zh: 这个例子有些微妙。对象 U 并不是一个类型，但 Lean 会自动把它强制转换为类型，方式是把它解释为 V 的子类型。因此，上面的例子可以更显式地重述为：

text: An important benefit of having a type Submodule K V instead of a predicate IsSubmodule : Set V → Prop is that one can easily endow Submodule K V with additional structure. Importantly, it has the structure of a complete lattice structure with respect to inclusion. For instance, instead of having a lemma stating that an intersection of two subspaces of V is again a subspace, we use the lattice operation ⊓ to construct the intersection. We can then apply arbitrary lemmas about lattices to the construction.
html: An important benefit of having a type Submodule K V instead of a predicate IsSubmodule : Set V → Prop is that one can easily endow Submodule K V with additional structure. Importantly, it has the structure of a complete lattice structure with respect to inclusion. For instance, instead of having a lemma stating that an intersection of two subspaces of V is again a subspace, we use the lattice operation ⊓ to construct the intersection. We can then apply arbitrary lemmas about lattices to the construction.
zh: 使用类型 Submodule K V 而不是谓词 IsSubmodule : Set V → Prop 的一个重要好处，是可以很容易地给 Submodule K V 赋予额外结构。尤其重要的是，按包含关系它具有完备格结构。例如，我们不会使用一个断言 V 的两个子空间的交仍为子空间的引理，而是使用格运算 ⊓ 来构造交。随后便可以对这一构造应用任意关于格的引理。

text: Let us check that the set underlying the infimum of two subspaces is indeed, by definition, their intersection.
html: Let us check that the set underlying the infimum of two subspaces is indeed, by definition, their intersection.
zh: 让我们检查两个子空间的下确界的底层集合确实按定义就是它们的交。

text: It may look strange to have a different notation for what amounts to the intersection of the underlying sets, but the correspondence does not carry over to the supremum operation and set union, since a union of subspaces is not, in general, a subspace. Instead one needs to use the subspace generated by the union, which is done using Submodule.span .
html: It may look strange to have a different notation for what amounts to the intersection of the underlying sets, but the correspondence does not carry over to the supremum operation and set union, since a union of subspaces is not, in general, a subspace. Instead one needs to use the subspace generated by the union, which is done using Submodule.span.
zh: 对于底层集合的交使用一个不同记号，看起来也许有些奇怪；但这种对应并不能延伸到上确界运算与集合并，因为子空间的并通常不是子空间。相反，需要使用由并集生成的子空间，这由 Submodule.span 完成。

text: Another subtlety is that V itself does not have type Submodule K V , so we need a way to talk about V seen as a subspace of V . This is also provided by the lattice structure: the full subspace is the top element of this lattice.
html: Another subtlety is that V itself does not have type Submodule K V, so we need a way to talk about V seen as a subspace of V. This is also provided by the lattice structure: the full subspace is the top element of this lattice.
zh: 另一个微妙点是，V 本身并不具有类型 Submodule K V，所以需要一种方式来谈论把 V 视为 V 的子空间。这同样由格结构提供：全子空间就是这个格的顶元。

text: Similarly the bottom element of this lattice is the subspace whose only element is the zero element.
html: Similarly the bottom element of this lattice is the subspace whose only element is the zero element.
zh: 类似地，这个格的底元是唯一元素为零元素的子空间。

text: In particular we can discuss the case of subspaces that are in (internal) direct sum. In the case of two subspaces, we use the general purpose predicate IsCompl which makes sense for any bounded partially ordered type. In the case of general families of subspaces we use DirectSum.IsInternal .
html: In particular we can discuss the case of subspaces that are in (internal) direct sum. In the case of two subspaces, we use the general purpose predicate IsCompl which makes sense for any bounded partially ordered type. In the case of general families of subspaces we use DirectSum.IsInternal.
zh: 特别地，我们可以讨论若干子空间构成（内部）直和的情形。对于两个子空间，使用通用谓词 IsCompl；它适用于任何有界偏序类型。对于一般的子空间族，则使用 DirectSum.IsInternal。

text: In addition to building subspaces out of existing subspaces, we can build them out of any set s using Submodule.span K s which builds the smallest subspace containing s . On paper it is common to use that this space is made of all linear combinations of elements of s . But it is often more efficient to use its universal property expressed by Submodule.span_le , and the whole theory of Galois connections.
html: In addition to building subspaces out of existing subspaces, we can build them out of any set s using Submodule.span K s which builds the smallest subspace containing s. On paper it is common to use that this space is made of all linear combinations of elements of s. But it is often more efficient to use its universal property expressed by Submodule.span_le, and the whole theory of Galois connections.
zh: 除了从已有子空间构造子空间之外，还可以从任意集合 s 出发，使用 Submodule.span K s 构造包含 s 的最小子空间。纸笔数学中常说这个空间由 s 中元素的所有线性组合组成。但在形式化中，更高效的做法往往是使用由 Submodule.span_le 表达的泛性质，以及伽罗瓦连接的整个理论。

text: When those are not enough, one can use the relevant induction principle Submodule.span_induction which ensures a property holds for every element of the span of s as long as it holds on zero and elements of s and is stable under sum and scalar multiplication.
html: When those are not enough, one can use the relevant induction principle Submodule.span_induction which ensures a property holds for every element of the span of s as long as it holds on zero and elements of s and is stable under sum and scalar multiplication.
zh: 当这些还不够时，可以使用相应的归纳原理 Submodule.span_induction。它保证：只要某个性质对零、对 s 中元素成立，并且在加法和标量乘法下稳定，那么它就对 s 的张成子空间中的每个元素成立。

text: As an exercise, let us reprove one implication of Submodule.mem_sup . Remember that you can use the module tactic to close goals that follow from the axioms relating the various algebraic operations on V .
html: As an exercise, let us reprove one implication of Submodule.mem_sup. Remember that you can use the module tactic to close goals that follow from the axioms relating the various algebraic operations on V.
zh: 作为练习，我们重新证明 Submodule.mem_sup 的一个方向。请记住，可以使用 module 策略关闭那些由 V 上各代数运算的公理推出的目标。

text: As promised earlier, we now describe how to push and pull subspaces by linear maps. As usual in Mathlib, the first operation is called map and the second one is called comap .
html: As promised earlier, we now describe how to push and pull subspaces by linear maps. As usual in Mathlib, the first operation is called map and the second one is called comap.
zh: 如前所述，现在说明如何用线性映射推出和拉回子空间。照 Mathlib 的惯例，第一个操作称为 map，第二个操作称为 comap。

text: Note those live in the Submodule namespace so one can use dot notation and write E.map φ instead of Submodule.map φ E , but this is pretty awkward to read (although some Mathlib contributors use this spelling).
html: Note those live in the Submodule namespace so one can use dot notation and write E.map φ instead of Submodule.map φ E, but this is pretty awkward to read (although some Mathlib contributors use this spelling).
zh: 注意，这些操作位于 Submodule 命名空间中，因此可以使用点记号，把 E.map φ 写成 Submodule.map φ E 的形式；不过这样读起来相当别扭（尽管一些 Mathlib 贡献者确实采用这种写法）。

text: In particular the range and kernel of a linear map are subspaces. Those special cases are important enough to get declarations.
html: In particular the range and kernel of a linear map are subspaces. Those special cases are important enough to get declarations.
zh: 特别地，线性映射的值域和核都是子空间。这些特殊情形足够重要，因此有专门的声明。

text: Note that we cannot write φ.ker instead of LinearMap.ker φ because LinearMap.ker also applies to classes of maps preserving more structure, hence it does not expect an argument whose type starts with LinearMap , hence dot notation doesn’t work here. However we were able to use the other flavor of dot notation in the right-hand side. Because Lean expects a term with type Submodule K V after elaborating the left-hand side, it interprets .comap as Submodule.comap .
html: Note that we cannot write φ.ker instead of LinearMap.ker φ because LinearMap.ker also applies to classes of maps preserving more structure, hence it does not expect an argument whose type starts with LinearMap, hence dot notation doesn’t work here. However we were able to use the other flavor of dot notation in the right-hand side. Because Lean expects a term with type Submodule K V after elaborating the left-hand side, it interprets .comap as Submodule.comap.
zh: 注意，不能把 LinearMap.ker φ 写成 φ.ker，因为 LinearMap.ker 还适用于保持更多结构的映射类，所以它并不期待一个类型以 LinearMap 开始的参数，点记号在这里无法工作。然而，在等式右端我们可以使用另一种点记号。由于在 elaboration 左端之后，Lean 期待一个类型为 Submodule K V 的项，它会把 .comap 解释为 Submodule.comap。

text: The following lemmas give the key relations between those submodule and the properties of φ .
html: The following lemmas give the key relations between those submodule and the properties of φ.
zh: 下面的引理给出这些子模与 φ 的性质之间的关键关系。

text: As an exercise, let us prove the Galois connection property for map and comap . One can use the following lemmas but this is not required since they are true by definition.
html: As an exercise, let us prove the Galois connection property for map and comap. One can use the following lemmas but this is not required since they are true by definition.
zh: 作为练习，我们证明 map 与 comap 的伽罗瓦连接性质。可以使用下面的引理，但这并非必要，因为这些结论按定义即成立。

text: Quotient vector spaces use the general quotient notation (typed with \quot , not the ordinary / ). The projection onto a quotient space is Submodule.mkQ and the universal property is Submodule.liftQ .
html: Quotient vector spaces use the general quotient notation (typed with \quot, not the ordinary /). The projection onto a quotient space is Submodule.mkQ and the universal property is Submodule.liftQ.
zh: 商向量空间使用一般的商记号（输入 \quot，而不是通常的 /）。到商空间的投影是 Submodule.mkQ，其泛性质是 Submodule.liftQ。

text: As an exercise, let us prove the correspondence theorem for subspaces of quotient spaces. Mathlib knows a slightly more precise version as Submodule.comapMkQRelIso .
html: As an exercise, let us prove the correspondence theorem for subspaces of quotient spaces. Mathlib knows a slightly more precise version as Submodule.comapMkQRelIso.
zh: 作为练习，我们证明商空间子空间的对应定理。Mathlib 中有一个稍微更精确的版本，称为 Submodule.comapMkQRelIso。

text: An important special case of linear maps are endomorphisms: linear maps from a vector space to itself. They are interesting because they form a K -algebra. In particular we can evaluate polynomials with coefficients in K on them, and they can have eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
html: An important special case of linear maps are endomorphisms: linear maps from a vector space to itself. They are interesting because they form a K-algebra. In particular we can evaluate polynomials with coefficients in K on them, and they can have eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
zh: 线性映射的一个重要特例是自同态：从一个向量空间到自身的线性映射。它们很有趣，因为它们形成一个 K-代数。特别地，可以把 K 系数多项式代入其中，也可以讨论它们的特征值和特征向量。

text: Mathlib uses the abbreviation Module.End K V := V →ₗ[K] V which is convenient when using a lot of these (especially after opening the Module namespace).
html: Mathlib uses the abbreviation Module.End K V := V →ₗ[K] V which is convenient when using a lot of these (especially after opening the Module namespace).
zh: Mathlib 使用缩写 Module.End K V := V →ₗ[K] V；当大量使用这类对象时，这很方便（尤其是在打开 Module 命名空间之后）。

text: As an exercise manipulating endomorphisms, subspaces and polynomials, let us prove the (binary) kernels lemma: for any endomorphism \(φ\) and any two relatively prime polynomials \(P\) and \(Q\) , we have \(\ker P(φ) ⊕ \ker Q(φ) = \ker \big(PQ(φ)\big)\) .
html: As an exercise manipulating endomorphisms, subspaces and polynomials, let us prove the (binary) kernels lemma: for any endomorphism \(φ\) and any two relatively prime polynomials \(P\) and \(Q\), we have \(\ker P(φ) ⊕ \ker Q(φ) = \ker \big(PQ(φ)\big)\).
zh: 作为一个操作自同态、子空间和多项式的练习，我们证明（二元）核引理：对任意自同态 \(φ\) 以及任意两个互素多项式 \(P\) 和 \(Q\)，有 \(\ker P(φ) ⊕ \ker Q(φ) = \ker \big(PQ(φ)\big)\)。

text: Note that IsCoprime x y is defined as ∃ a b, a * x + b * y = 1 .
html: Note that IsCoprime x y is defined as ∃ a b, a * x + b * y = 1.
zh: 注意，IsCoprime x y 被定义为 ∃ a b, a * x + b * y = 1。

text: We now move to the discussions of eigenspaces and eigenvalues. The eigenspace associated to an endomorphism \(φ\) and a scalar \(a\) is the kernel of \(φ - aId\) . Eigenspaces are defined for all values of a , although they are interesting only when they are non-zero. However an eigenvector is, by definition, a non-zero element of an eigenspace. The corresponding predicate is End.HasEigenvector .
html: We now move to the discussions of eigenspaces and eigenvalues. The eigenspace associated to an endomorphism \(φ\) and a scalar \(a\) is the kernel of \(φ - aId\). Eigenspaces are defined for all values of a, although they are interesting only when they are non-zero. However an eigenvector is, by definition, a non-zero element of an eigenspace. The corresponding predicate is End.HasEigenvector.
zh: 现在转向特征空间与特征值的讨论。与自同态 \(φ\) 和标量 \(a\) 对应的特征空间，是 \(φ - aId\) 的核。特征空间对所有 a 都有定义，尽管只有非零时才有意义。不过，特征向量按定义是特征空间中的非零元素。相应的谓词是 End.HasEigenvector。

text: Then there is a predicate End.HasEigenvalue and the corresponding subtype End.Eigenvalues .
html: Then there is a predicate End.HasEigenvalue and the corresponding subtype End.Eigenvalues.
zh: 此外还有谓词 End.HasEigenvalue，以及对应的子类型 End.Eigenvalues。

text: Before introducing bases for abstract vector spaces, we go back to the much more elementary setup of linear algebra in \(K^n\) for some field \(K\) . Here the main objects are vectors and matrices. For concrete vectors, one can use the ![…] notation, where components are separated by commas. For concrete matrices we can use the !![…] notation, rows are separated by semicolons and elements of the rows are separated by commas. When entries have a computable type such as ℕ or ℚ , we can use the eval command to play with basic operations.
html: Before introducing bases for abstract vector spaces, we go back to the much more elementary setup of linear algebra in \(K^n\) for some field \(K\). Here the main objects are vectors and matrices. For concrete vectors, one can use the ![…] notation, where components are separated by commas. For concrete matrices we can use the !![…] notation, rows are separated by semicolons and elements of the rows are separated by commas. When entries have a computable type such as ℕ or ℚ, we can use the eval command to play with basic operations.
zh: 在介绍抽象向量空间的基之前，我们回到更初等的设置：域 \(K\) 上的 \(K^n\) 中的线性代数。这里的主要对象是向量和矩阵。对具体向量，可以使用 ![…] 记号，其中分量用逗号分隔。对具体矩阵，可以使用 !![…] 记号，行之间用分号分隔，每行中的元素用逗号分隔。当项具有可计算类型，例如 ℕ 或 ℚ 时，可以使用 eval 命令尝试基本运算。

text: It is important to understand that this use of #eval is interesting only for exploration, it is not meant to replace a computer algebra system such as Sage. The data representation used here for matrices is not computationally efficient in any way. It uses functions instead of arrays and is optimized for proving, not computing. The virtual machine used by #eval is also not optimized for this use.
html: It is important to understand that this use of #eval is interesting only for exploration, it is not meant to replace a computer algebra system such as Sage. The data representation used here for matrices is not computationally efficient in any way. It uses functions instead of arrays and is optimized for proving, not computing. The virtual machine used by #eval is also not optimized for this use.
zh: 必须理解，这里使用 #eval 只对探索有意义，并不意在取代 Sage 之类的计算机代数系统。这里用于矩阵的数据表示在计算上完全不高效。它使用函数而不是数组，并针对证明而非计算优化。#eval 所使用的虚拟机也并未为这种用途优化。

text: Beware the matrix notation list rows but the vector notation is neither a row vector nor a column vector. Multiplication of a matrix with a vector from the left (resp. right) interprets the vector as a row (resp. column) vector. This corresponds to operations Matrix.vecMul , with notation ᵥ* and Matrix.mulVec , with notation ` *ᵥ` . Those notations are scoped in the Matrix namespace that we therefore need to open.
html: Beware the matrix notation list rows but the vector notation is neither a row vector nor a column vector. Multiplication of a matrix with a vector from the left (resp. right) interprets the vector as a row (resp. column) vector. This corresponds to operations Matrix.vecMul, with notation ᵥ* and Matrix.mulVec, with notation ` *ᵥ`. Those notations are scoped in the Matrix namespace that we therefore need to open.
zh: 注意，矩阵记号列出的是行，但向量记号既不是行向量也不是列向量。矩阵从左侧（相应地，从右侧）与向量相乘时，会把该向量解释为行向量（相应地，列向量）。这对应于操作 Matrix.vecMul（记号为 ᵥ*）和 Matrix.mulVec（记号为 *ᵥ`）。这些记号限定在 Matrix 命名空间中，因此我们需要打开该命名空间。

text: In order to generate matrices with identical rows or columns specified by a vector, we use Matrix.replicateRow and Matrix.replicateCol , with arguments the type indexing the rows or columns and the vector. For instance one can get single row or single column matrixes (more precisely matrices whose rows or columns are indexed by Fin 1 ).
html: In order to generate matrices with identical rows or columns specified by a vector, we use Matrix.replicateRow and Matrix.replicateCol, with arguments the type indexing the rows or columns and the vector. For instance one can get single row or single column matrixes (more precisely matrices whose rows or columns are indexed by Fin 1).
zh: 为了生成由某个向量指定、且各行或各列相同的矩阵，可以使用 Matrix.replicateRow 和 Matrix.replicateCol；其参数为行或列的指标类型以及该向量。例如，可以得到单行或单列矩阵（更准确地说，是行或列由 Fin 1 作指标的矩阵）。

text: Other familiar operations include the vector dot product, matrix transpose, and, for square matrices, determinant and trace.
html: Other familiar operations include the vector dot product, matrix transpose, and, for square matrices, determinant and trace.
zh: 其他熟悉的运算包括向量点积、矩阵转置，以及方阵的行列式和迹。

text: When entries do not have a computable type, for instance if they are real numbers, we cannot hope that #eval can help. Also this kind of evaluation cannot be used in proofs without considerably expanding the trusted code base (i.e. the part of Lean that you need to trust when checking proofs).
html: When entries do not have a computable type, for instance if they are real numbers, we cannot hope that #eval can help. Also this kind of evaluation cannot be used in proofs without considerably expanding the trusted code base (i.e. the part of Lean that you need to trust when checking proofs).
zh: 当项没有可计算类型时，例如它们是实数，我们不能指望 #eval 提供帮助。此外，这类求值若要用于证明，会显著扩大可信代码基，即检查证明时必须信任的那部分 Lean。

text: So it is good to also use the simp and norm_num tactics in proofs, or their command counter-part for quick exploration.
html: So it is good to also use the simp and norm_num tactics in proofs, or their command counter-part for quick exploration.
zh: 因此，在证明中也应使用 simp 和 norm_num 策略；若只是快速探索，则可使用它们对应的命令形式。

text: The next important operation on square matrices is inversion. In the same way as division of numbers is always defined and returns the artificial value zero for division by zero, the inversion operation is defined on all matrices and returns the zero matrix for non-invertible matrices.
html: The next important operation on square matrices is inversion. In the same way as division of numbers is always defined and returns the artificial value zero for division by zero, the inversion operation is defined on all matrices and returns the zero matrix for non-invertible matrices.
zh: 方阵的下一个重要运算是求逆。正如数的除法总是有定义，并且在除以零时返回人为指定的值零一样，矩阵求逆也对所有矩阵有定义，并在矩阵不可逆时返回零矩阵。

text: More precisely, there is general function Ring.inverse that does this in any ring, and, for any matrix A , A⁻¹ is defined as Ring.inverse A.det • A.adjugate . According to Cramer’s rule, this is indeed the inverse of A when the determinant of A is not zero.
html: More precisely, there is general function Ring.inverse that does this in any ring, and, for any matrix A, A⁻¹ is defined as Ring.inverse A.det • A.adjugate. According to Cramer’s rule, this is indeed the inverse of A when the determinant of A is not zero.
zh: 更精确地说，有一个一般函数 Ring.inverse，它在任意环中完成这一点；对任意矩阵 A，A⁻¹ 被定义为 Ring.inverse A.det • A.adjugate。根据克拉默法则，当 A 的行列式不为零时，这确实是 A 的逆。

text: Of course this definition is really useful only for invertible matrices. There is a general type class Invertible that helps recording this. For instance, the simp call in the next example will use the inv_mul_of_invertible lemma which has an Invertible type-class assumption, so it will trigger only if this can be found by the type-class synthesis system. Here we make this fact available using a have statement.
html: Of course this definition is really useful only for invertible matrices. There is a general type class Invertible that helps recording this. For instance, the simp call in the next example will use the inv_mul_of_invertible lemma which has an Invertible type-class assumption, so it will trigger only if this can be found by the type-class synthesis system. Here we make this fact available using a have statement.
zh: 当然，这个定义真正有用的情形是可逆矩阵。有一个一般类型类 Invertible 可用于记录这一事实。例如，下一个例子中的 simp 调用会使用引理 inv_mul_of_invertible，而该引理有一个 Invertible 类型类假设；因此只有当类型类合成系统能找到它时，该引理才会触发。这里我们用一个 have 语句使这一事实可用。

text: In this fully concrete case, we could also use the norm_num machinery, and apply? to find the final line:
html: In this fully concrete case, we could also use the norm_num machinery, and apply? to find the final line:
zh: 在这个完全具体的情形中，也可以使用 norm_num 机制，并用 apply? 找到最后一行：

text: All the concrete matrices above have their rows and columns indexed by Fin n for some n (not necessarily the same for rows and columns). But sometimes it is more convenient to index matrices using arbitrary finite types. For instance the adjacency matrix of a finite graph has rows and columns naturally indexed by the vertices of the graph.
html: All the concrete matrices above have their rows and columns indexed by Fin n for some n (not necessarily the same for rows and columns). But sometimes it is more convenient to index matrices using arbitrary finite types. For instance the adjacency matrix of a finite graph has rows and columns naturally indexed by the vertices of the graph.
zh: 上面所有具体矩阵的行和列都由某个 n 的 Fin n 作指标（行与列的大小不必相同）。但有时用任意有限类型给矩阵作指标更方便。例如，有限图的邻接矩阵，其行和列自然由图的顶点作指标。

text: In fact, when one simply wants to define matrices without defining any operation on them, finiteness of the indexing types are not even needed, and coefficients can have any type, without any algebraic structure. So Mathlib simply defines Matrix m n α to be m → n → α for any types m , n and α , and the matrices we have been using so far had types such as Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ . Of course algebraic operations require more assumptions on m , n and α .
html: In fact, when one simply wants to define matrices without defining any operation on them, finiteness of the indexing types are not even needed, and coefficients can have any type, without any algebraic structure. So Mathlib simply defines Matrix m n α to be m → n → α for any types m, n and α, and the matrices we have been using so far had types such as Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ. Of course algebraic operations require more assumptions on m, n and α.
zh: 事实上，如果只是想定义矩阵而不定义其上的任何运算，甚至不需要指标类型有限，系数也可以是任意类型，不必有任何代数结构。因此，Mathlib 简单地把 Matrix m n α 定义为 m → n → α，其中 m、n 和 α 是任意类型；到目前为止我们使用的矩阵具有诸如 Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℝ 这样的类型。当然，代数运算需要对 m、n 和 α 作更多假设。

text: Note the main reason why we do not use m → n → α directly is to allow the type class system to understand what we want. For instance, for a ring R , the type n → R is endowed with the point-wise multiplication operation, and similarly m → n → R has this operation which is not the multiplication we want on matrices.
html: Note the main reason why we do not use m → n → α directly is to allow the type class system to understand what we want. For instance, for a ring R, the type n → R is endowed with the point-wise multiplication operation, and similarly m → n → R has this operation which is not the multiplication we want on matrices.
zh: 注意，我们不直接使用 m → n → α 的主要原因，是为了让类型类系统理解我们的意图。例如，对一个环 R，类型 n → R 带有逐点乘法；类似地，m → n → R 也带有这种运算，而这不是我们希望用于矩阵的乘法。

text: In the first example below, we force Lean to see through the definition of Matrix and accept the statement as meaningful, and then prove it by checking all entries.
html: In the first example below, we force Lean to see through the definition of Matrix and accept the statement as meaningful, and then prove it by checking all entries.
zh: 在下面第一个例子中，我们强制 Lean 展开 Matrix 的定义，使其接受该命题有意义，然后通过检查所有项来证明它。

text: But then the next two examples reveal that Lean uses the point-wise multiplication on Fin 2 → Fin 2 → ℤ but the matrix multiplication on Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ .
html: But then the next two examples reveal that Lean uses the point-wise multiplication on Fin 2 → Fin 2 → ℤ but the matrix multiplication on Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ.
zh: 但接下来的两个例子显示，Lean 对 Fin 2 → Fin 2 → ℤ 使用逐点乘法，而对 Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℤ 使用矩阵乘法。

text: In order to define matrices as functions without losing the benefits of Matrix for type class synthesis, we can use the equivalence Matrix.of between functions and matrices. This equivalence is secretly defined using Equiv.refl .
html: In order to define matrices as functions without losing the benefits of Matrix for type class synthesis, we can use the equivalence Matrix.of between functions and matrices. This equivalence is secretly defined using Equiv.refl.
zh: 为了把矩阵定义为函数，同时又不丢失 Matrix 对类型类合成带来的好处，可以使用函数与矩阵之间的等价 Matrix.of。这个等价在背后是用 Equiv.refl 定义的。

text: For instance we can define Vandermonde matrices corresponding to a vector v .
html: For instance we can define Vandermonde matrices corresponding to a vector v.
zh: 例如，可以定义与向量 v 对应的范德蒙德矩阵。

text: We now want to discuss bases of vector spaces. Informally there are many ways to define this notion. One can use a universal property. One can say a basis is a family of vectors that is linearly independent and spanning. Or one can combine those properties and directly say that a basis is a family of vectors such that every vectors can be written uniquely as a linear combination of bases vectors. Yet another way to say it is that a basis provides a linear isomorphism with a power of the base field K , seen as a vector space over K .
html: We now want to discuss bases of vector spaces. Informally there are many ways to define this notion. One can use a universal property. One can say a basis is a family of vectors that is linearly independent and spanning. Or one can combine those properties and directly say that a basis is a family of vectors such that every vectors can be written uniquely as a linear combination of bases vectors. Yet another way to say it is that a basis provides a linear isomorphism with a power of the base field K, seen as a vector space over K.
zh: 现在讨论向量空间的基。非形式化地说，这一概念有多种定义方式。可以使用泛性质；可以说基是一族线性无关且张成全空间的向量；也可以把这些性质合并，直接说基是一族向量，使得每个向量都能唯一地写成基向量的线性组合。还有一种说法是，基给出了与基域 K 的幂的线性同构，其中 K 被视为自身上的向量空间。

text: This isomorphism version is actually the one that Mathlib uses as a definition under the hood, and other characterizations are proven from it. One must be slightly careful with the “power of K ” idea in the case of infinite bases. Indeed, only finite linear combinations make sense in this algebraic context. So what we need as a reference vector space is not a direct product of copies of K but a direct sum. We could use ⨁ i : ι, K for some type ι indexing the basis, but instead we use the more specialized spelling ι →₀ K , which means “functions from ι to K with finite support”, i.e. functions which vanish outside a finite set in ι (this finite set is not fixed, it depends on the function). Evaluating such a function coming from a basis B at a vector v and i : ι returns the component (or coordinate) of v on the i -th basis vector.
html: This isomorphism version is actually the one that Mathlib uses as a definition under the hood, and other characterizations are proven from it. One must be slightly careful with the “power of K” idea in the case of infinite bases. Indeed, only finite linear combinations make sense in this algebraic context. So what we need as a reference vector space is not a direct product of copies of K but a direct sum. We could use ⨁ i : ι, K for some type ι indexing the basis, but instead we use the more specialized spelling ι →₀ K, which means “functions from ι to K with finite support”, i.e. functions which vanish outside a finite set in ι (this finite set is not fixed, it depends on the function). Evaluating such a function coming from a basis B at a vector v and i : ι returns the component (or coordinate) of v on the i-th basis vector.
zh: 这个同构版本实际上是 Mathlib 底层采用的定义，其他刻画则由此证明出来。对于“K 的幂”这一想法，在无限基的情形中必须稍加谨慎。事实上，在这个代数语境中，只有有限线性组合有意义。因此，我们需要的参照向量空间不是若干份 K 的直积，而是直和。若某个类型 ι 为基作指标，可以使用 ⨁ i : ι, K；但实际采用的是更专门的写法 ι →₀ K，意思是“从 ι 到 K 的有限支撑函数”，即在 ι 的某个有限集之外取零的函数（这个有限集不是固定的，而依赖于具体函数）。若这样的函数来自基 B，在向量 v 和 i : ι 处求值，会返回 v 在第 i 个基向量上的分量（或坐标）。

text: The type of bases indexed by a type ι of V as a K vector space is Basis ι K V . The isomorphism is called Basis.repr .
html: The type of bases indexed by a type ι of V as a K vector space is Basis ι K V. The isomorphism is called Basis.repr.
zh: 以类型 ι 为指标的 V 作为 K-向量空间的基的类型是 Basis ι K V。相应的同构称为 Basis.repr。

text: Instead of starting with such an isomorphism, one can start with a family b of vectors that is linearly independent and spanning, this is Basis.mk .
html: Instead of starting with such an isomorphism, one can start with a family b of vectors that is linearly independent and spanning, this is Basis.mk.
zh: 不从这种同构出发，也可以从一族线性无关且张成全空间的向量 b 出发；这就是 Basis.mk。

text: The assumption that the family is spanning is spelled out as ⊤ ≤ Submodule.span K (Set.range b) . Here ⊤ is the top submodule of V , i.e. V seen as submodule of itself. This spelling looks a bit tortuous, but we will see below that it is almost equivalent by definition to the more readable ∀ v, v ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range b) (the underscores in the snippet below refers to the useless information v ∈ ⊤ ).
html: The assumption that the family is spanning is spelled out as ⊤ ≤ Submodule.span K (Set.range b). Here ⊤ is the top submodule of V, i.e. V seen as submodule of itself. This spelling looks a bit tortuous, but we will see below that it is almost equivalent by definition to the more readable ∀ v, v ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range b) (the underscores in the snippet below refers to the useless information v ∈ ⊤).
zh: 这族向量张成全空间的假设写作 ⊤ ≤ Submodule.span K (Set.range b)。这里 ⊤ 是 V 的顶子模，也就是把 V 视为自身的子模。这种写法看起来有些曲折，但下面会看到，它几乎按定义等价于更易读的 ∀ v, v ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range b)（下面代码片段中的下划线对应无用的信息 v ∈ ⊤）。

text: In particular the model vector space ι →₀ K has a so-called canonical basis whose repr function evaluated on any vector is the identity isomorphism. It is called Finsupp.basisSingleOne where Finsupp means function with finite support and basisSingleOne refers to the fact that basis vectors are functions which vanish except for a single input value. More precisely the basis vector indexed by i : ι is Finsupp.single i 1 which is the finitely supported function taking value 1 at i and 0 everywhere else.
html: In particular the model vector space ι →₀ K has a so-called canonical basis whose repr function evaluated on any vector is the identity isomorphism. It is called Finsupp.basisSingleOne where Finsupp means function with finite support and basisSingleOne refers to the fact that basis vectors are functions which vanish except for a single input value. More precisely the basis vector indexed by i : ι is Finsupp.single i 1 which is the finitely supported function taking value 1 at i and 0 everywhere else.
zh: 特别地，模型向量空间 ι →₀ K 有一个所谓的标准基；其 repr 函数作用于任意向量时就是恒等同构。它称为 Finsupp.basisSingleOne，其中 Finsupp 表示有限支撑函数，而 basisSingleOne 指基向量是在除单个输入值以外处都为零的函数。更精确地说，由 i : ι 作指标的基向量是 Finsupp.single i 1，即在 i 处取值 1、在其他地方取值 0 的有限支撑函数。

text: The story of finitely supported functions is unneeded when the indexing type is finite. In this case we can use the simpler Pi.basisFun which gives a basis of the whole ι → K .
html: The story of finitely supported functions is unneeded when the indexing type is finite. In this case we can use the simpler Pi.basisFun which gives a basis of the whole ι → K.
zh: 当指标类型有限时，不需要有限支撑函数的这些讨论。在这种情况下，可以使用更简单的 Pi.basisFun，它给出整个 ι → K 的一组基。

text: Going back to the general case of bases of abstract vector spaces, we can express any vector as a linear combination of basis vectors. Let us first see the easy case of finite bases.
html: Going back to the general case of bases of abstract vector spaces, we can express any vector as a linear combination of basis vectors. Let us first see the easy case of finite bases.
zh: 回到抽象向量空间基的一般情形，可以把任意向量表示为基向量的线性组合。先看有限基的简单情形。

text: When ι is not finite, the above statement makes no sense a priori: we cannot take a sum over ι . However the support of the function being summed is finite (it is the support of B.repr v ). But we need to apply a construction that takes this into account. Here Mathlib uses a special purpose function that requires some time to get used to: Finsupp.linearCombination (which is built on top of the more general Finsupp.sum ). Given a finitely supported function c from a type ι to the base field K and any function f from ι to V , Finsupp.linearCombination K f c is the sum over the support of c of the scalar multiplication c • f . In particular, we can replace it by a sum over any finite set containing the support of c .
html: When ι is not finite, the above statement makes no sense a priori: we cannot take a sum over ι. However the support of the function being summed is finite (it is the support of B.repr v). But we need to apply a construction that takes this into account. Here Mathlib uses a special purpose function that requires some time to get used to: Finsupp.linearCombination (which is built on top of the more general Finsupp.sum). Given a finitely supported function c from a type ι to the base field K and any function f from ι to V, Finsupp.linearCombination K f c is the sum over the support of c of the scalar multiplication c • f. In particular, we can replace it by a sum over any finite set containing the support of c.
zh: 当 ι 不是有限类型时，上面的陈述先验上没有意义：不能在 ι 上取和。然而，被求和函数的支撑是有限的（它就是 B.repr v 的支撑）。因此需要使用能体现这一点的构造。这里 Mathlib 使用一个需要花时间熟悉的专门函数：Finsupp.linearCombination（它建立在更一般的 Finsupp.sum 之上）。给定一个从类型 ι 到基域 K 的有限支撑函数 c，以及任意函数 f 从 ι 到 V，Finsupp.linearCombination K f c 是在 c 的支撑上对标量乘法 c • f 求和。特别地，可以把它替换成在任意包含 c 支撑的有限集上求和。

text: One could also assume that f is finitely supported and still get a well defined sum. But the choice made by Finsupp.linearCombination is the one relevant to our basis discussion since it allows to state the generalization of Basis.sum_repr .
html: One could also assume that f is finitely supported and still get a well defined sum. But the choice made by Finsupp.linearCombination is the one relevant to our basis discussion since it allows to state the generalization of Basis.sum_repr.
zh: 也可以假设 f 是有限支撑的，并仍然得到一个良定义的和。但 Finsupp.linearCombination 所作的选择才与这里关于基的讨论相关，因为它允许陈述 Basis.sum_repr 的一般化版本。

text: One could wonder why K is an explicit argument here, despite the fact it can be inferred from the type of c . The point is that the partially applied Finsupp.linearCombination K f is interesting in itself. It is not a bare function from ι →₀ K to V but a K -linear map.
html: One could wonder why K is an explicit argument here, despite the fact it can be inferred from the type of c. The point is that the partially applied Finsupp.linearCombination K f is interesting in itself. It is not a bare function from ι →₀ K to V but a K-linear map.
zh: 也许会疑惑，为什么这里 K 是显式参数，尽管它可以从 c 的类型推断出来。关键在于，部分应用后的 Finsupp.linearCombination K f 本身是有意义的对象。它不是一个从 ι →₀ K 到 V 的普通函数，而是一个 K-线性映射。

text: Returning to the mathematical discussion, it is important to understand that the representation of vectors in a basis is less useful in formalized mathematics than you may think. Indeed it is very often more efficient to directly use more abstract properties of bases. In particular the universal property of bases connecting them to other free objects in algebra allows to construct linear maps by specifying the images of basis vectors. This is Basis.constr . For any K -vector space W , our basis B gives a linear isomorphism Basis.constr B K from ι → W to V →ₗ[K] W . This isomorphism is characterized by the fact that it sends any function u : ι → W to a linear map sending the basis vector B i to u i , for every i : ι .
html: Returning to the mathematical discussion, it is important to understand that the representation of vectors in a basis is less useful in formalized mathematics than you may think. Indeed it is very often more efficient to directly use more abstract properties of bases. In particular the universal property of bases connecting them to other free objects in algebra allows to construct linear maps by specifying the images of basis vectors. This is Basis.constr. For any K-vector space W, our basis B gives a linear isomorphism Basis.constr B K from ι → W to V →ₗ[K] W. This isomorphism is characterized by the fact that it sends any function u : ι → W to a linear map sending the basis vector B i to u i, for every i : ι.
zh: 回到数学讨论，需要理解的是，在形式化数学中，基下的向量表示并没有你可能想象的那样有用。事实上，直接使用基的更抽象性质往往高效得多。特别地，基与代数中其他自由对象相联系的泛性质允许通过指定基向量的像来构造线性映射。这就是 Basis.constr。对任意 K-向量空间 W，我们的基 B 给出从 ι → W 到 V →ₗ[K] W 的线性同构 Basis.constr B K。这个同构由如下性质刻画：它把任意函数 u : ι → W 送到一个线性映射；该线性映射对每个 i : ι 都把基向量 B i 送到 u i。

text: This property is indeed characteristic because linear maps are determined by their values on bases:
html: This property is indeed characteristic because linear maps are determined by their values on bases:
zh: 这个性质确实是刻画性的，因为线性映射由其在基上的取值决定：

text: If we also have a basis B' on the target space then we can identify linear maps with matrices. This identification is a K -linear isomorphism.
html: If we also have a basis B' on the target space then we can identify linear maps with matrices. This identification is a K-linear isomorphism.
zh: 如果目标空间上也有一组基 B'，那么可以把线性映射与矩阵等同起来。这个等同是一个 K-线性同构。

text: As an exercise on this topic, we will prove part of the theorem which guarantees that endomorphisms have a well-defined determinant. Namely we want to prove that when two bases are indexed by the same type, the matrices they attach to any endomorphism have the same determinant. This would then need to be complemented using that bases all have isomorphic indexing types to get the full result.
html: As an exercise on this topic, we will prove part of the theorem which guarantees that endomorphisms have a well-defined determinant. Namely we want to prove that when two bases are indexed by the same type, the matrices they attach to any endomorphism have the same determinant. This would then need to be complemented using that bases all have isomorphic indexing types to get the full result.
zh: 作为关于这一主题的练习，我们将证明保证自同态具有良定义行列式的定理的一部分。具体而言，我们要证明：当两组基由同一类型作指标时，它们附加到任意自同态上的矩阵具有相同的行列式。为了得到完整结果，还需要补充使用所有基的指标类型彼此同构这一事实。

text: Of course Mathlib already knows this, and simp can close the goal immediately, so you shouldn’t use it too soon, but rather use the provided lemmas.
html: Of course Mathlib already knows this, and simp can close the goal immediately, so you shouldn’t use it too soon, but rather use the provided lemmas.
zh: 当然，Mathlib 已经知道这一点，并且 simp 可以立即关闭目标；因此不要过早使用它，而应使用给定的引理。

text: Returning to the case of a single vector space, bases are also useful to define the concept of dimension. Here again, there is the elementary case of finite-dimensional vector spaces. For such spaces we expect a dimension which is a natural number. This is Module.finrank . It takes the base field as an explicit argument since a given abelian group can be a vector space over different fields.
html: Returning to the case of a single vector space, bases are also useful to define the concept of dimension. Here again, there is the elementary case of finite-dimensional vector spaces. For such spaces we expect a dimension which is a natural number. This is Module.finrank. It takes the base field as an explicit argument since a given abelian group can be a vector space over different fields.
zh: 回到单个向量空间的情形，基也可用于定义维数概念。这里同样有有限维向量空间的初等情形。对这类空间，我们期望维数是一个自然数。这就是 Module.finrank。它把基域作为显式参数，因为同一个交换群可以成为不同域上的向量空间。

text: Note that Module.finrank is defined for any vector space. It returns zero for infinite dimensional vector spaces, just as division by zero returns zero.
html: Note that Module.finrank is defined for any vector space. It returns zero for infinite dimensional vector spaces, just as division by zero returns zero.
zh: 注意，Module.finrank 对任意向量空间都有定义。对无限维向量空间，它返回零，正如除以零返回零一样。

text: Of course many lemmas require a finite dimension assumption. This is the role of the FiniteDimensional typeclass. For instance, think about how the next example fails without this assumption.
html: Of course many lemmas require a finite dimension assumption. This is the role of the FiniteDimensional typeclass. For instance, think about how the next example fails without this assumption.
zh: 当然，许多引理需要有限维假设。这正是类型类 FiniteDimensional 的作用。例如，请思考如果没有这个假设，下一个例子会如何失败。

text: In the above statement, Nontrivial V means V has at least two different elements. Note that Module.finrank_pos_iff has no explicit argument. This is fine when using it from left to right, but not when using it from right to left because Lean has no way to guess K from the statement Nontrivial V . In that case it is useful to use the name argument syntax, after checking that the lemma is stated over a ring named R . So we can write:
html: In the above statement, Nontrivial V means V has at least two different elements. Note that Module.finrank_pos_iff has no explicit argument. This is fine when using it from left to right, but not when using it from right to left because Lean has no way to guess K from the statement Nontrivial V. In that case it is useful to use the name argument syntax, after checking that the lemma is stated over a ring named R. So we can write:
zh: 在上面的陈述中，Nontrivial V 表示 V 至少有两个不同元素。注意，Module.finrank_pos_iff 没有显式参数。从左到右使用它时这没有问题；但从右到左使用时就不行，因为 Lean 无法从命题 Nontrivial V 猜出 K。此时，在检查该引理是关于一个名为 R 的环陈述之后，使用命名参数语法很有帮助。因此可以写作：

text: The above spelling is strange because we already have h as an assumption, so we could just as well give the full proof Module.finrank_pos_iff.1 h but it is good to know for more complicated cases.
html: The above spelling is strange because we already have h as an assumption, so we could just as well give the full proof Module.finrank_pos_iff.1 h but it is good to know for more complicated cases.
zh: 上面的写法有些奇怪，因为我们已经有假设 h，所以完全可以直接给出完整证明 Module.finrank_pos_iff.1 h；不过在更复杂的情形中，了解这种写法很有用。

text: By definition, FiniteDimensional K V can be read from any basis.
html: By definition, FiniteDimensional K V can be read from any basis.
zh: 按定义，FiniteDimensional K V 可以从任意一组基读出。

text: Using that the subtype corresponding to a linear subspace has a vector space structure, we can talk about the dimension of a subspace.
html: Using that the subtype corresponding to a linear subspace has a vector space structure, we can talk about the dimension of a subspace.
zh: 利用线性子空间对应的子类型具有向量空间结构这一事实，可以讨论子空间的维数。

text: In the first statement above, the purpose of the type ascriptions is to make sure that coercion to Type* does not trigger too early.
html: In the first statement above, the purpose of the type ascriptions is to make sure that coercion to Type* does not trigger too early.
zh: 在上面第一个陈述中，类型标注的目的，是确保到 Type* 的强制转换不会过早触发。

text: We are now ready for an exercise about finrank and subspaces.
html: We are now ready for an exercise about finrank and subspaces.
zh: 现在可以做一个关于 finrank 与子空间的练习。

text: Let us now move to the general case of dimension theory. In this case finrank is useless, but we still have that, for any two bases of the same vector space, there is a bijection between the types indexing those bases. So we can still hope to define the rank as a cardinal, i.e. an element of the “quotient of the collection of types under the existence of a bijection equivalence relation”.
html: Let us now move to the general case of dimension theory. In this case finrank is useless, but we still have that, for any two bases of the same vector space, there is a bijection between the types indexing those bases. So we can still hope to define the rank as a cardinal, i.e. an element of the “quotient of the collection of types under the existence of a bijection equivalence relation”.
zh: 现在转向维数理论的一般情形。在这种情况下，finrank 没有用；但我们仍然知道，同一向量空间的任意两组基，其指标类型之间存在一个双射。因此仍可希望把秩定义为一个基数，即“所有类型按存在双射这一等价关系取商”所得对象的一个元素。

text: When discussing cardinal, it gets harder to ignore foundational issues around Russel’s paradox like we do everywhere else in this book. There is no type of all types because it would lead to logical inconsistencies. This issue is solved by the hierarchy of universes that we usually try to ignore.
html: When discussing cardinal, it gets harder to ignore foundational issues around Russel’s paradox like we do everywhere else in this book. There is no type of all types because it would lead to logical inconsistencies. This issue is solved by the hierarchy of universes that we usually try to ignore.
zh: 讨论基数时，就更难像本书其他地方那样忽略与罗素悖论有关的基础问题。不存在所有类型组成的类型，因为那会导致逻辑不一致。这个问题由宇宙层级解决，而我们通常尽量不去显式处理它。

text: Each type has a universe level, and those levels behave similarly to natural numbers. In particular there is zeroth level, and the corresponding universe Type 0 is simply denoted by Type . This universe is enough to hold almost all of classical mathematics. For instance ℕ and ℝ have type Type . Each level u has a successor denoted by u + 1 , and Type u has type Type (u+1) .
html: Each type has a universe level, and those levels behave similarly to natural numbers. In particular there is zeroth level, and the corresponding universe Type 0 is simply denoted by Type. This universe is enough to hold almost all of classical mathematics. For instance ℕ and ℝ have type Type. Each level u has a successor denoted by u + 1, and Type u has type Type (u+1).
zh: 每个类型都有一个宇宙层级，这些层级的行为类似自然数。特别地，有第零层级，相应的宇宙 Type 0 简记为 Type。这个宇宙足以容纳几乎所有经典数学。例如，ℕ 和 ℝ 的类型都是 Type。每个层级 u 都有一个后继，记作 u + 1；并且 Type u 的类型是 Type (u+1)。

text: But universe levels are not natural numbers, they have a really different nature and don’t have a type. In particular you cannot state in Lean something like u ≠ u + 1 . There is simply no type where this would take place. Even stating Type u ≠ Type (u+1) does not make any sense since Type u and Type (u+1) have different types.
html: But universe levels are not natural numbers, they have a really different nature and don’t have a type. In particular you cannot state in Lean something like u ≠ u + 1. There is simply no type where this would take place. Even stating Type u ≠ Type (u+1) does not make any sense since Type u and Type (u+1) have different types.
zh: 但宇宙层级并不是自然数，它们具有完全不同的性质，并且自身没有类型。特别地，不能在 Lean 中陈述诸如 u ≠ u + 1 这样的命题。根本不存在一个使这句话可安放其中的类型。即使陈述 Type u ≠ Type (u+1) 也没有意义，因为 Type u 与 Type (u+1) 具有不同的类型。

text: Whenever we write Type* , Lean inserts a universe level variable named u_n where n is a number. This allows definitions and statements to live in all universes.
html: Whenever we write Type*, Lean inserts a universe level variable named u_n where n is a number. This allows definitions and statements to live in all universes.
zh: 每当写 Type* 时，Lean 都会插入一个名为 u_n 的宇宙层级变量，其中 n 是一个数字。这使定义和陈述能够生活在所有宇宙中。

text: Given a universe level u , we can define an equivalence relation on Type u saying two types α and β are equivalent if there is a bijection between them. The quotient type Cardinal.{u} lives in Type (u+1) . The curly braces denote a universe variable. The image of α : Type u in this quotient is Cardinal.mk α : Cardinal.{u} .
html: Given a universe level u, we can define an equivalence relation on Type u saying two types α and β are equivalent if there is a bijection between them. The quotient type Cardinal.{u} lives in Type (u+1). The curly braces denote a universe variable. The image of α : Type u in this quotient is Cardinal.mk α : Cardinal.{u}.
zh: 给定宇宙层级 u，可以在 Type u 上定义一个等价关系：若类型 α 与 β 之间存在双射，则称它们等价。商类型 Cardinal.{u} 生活在 Type (u+1) 中。花括号表示宇宙变量。α : Type u 在这个商中的像是 Cardinal.mk α : Cardinal.{u}。

text: But we cannot directly compare cardinals in different universes. So technically we cannot define the rank of a vector space V as the cardinal of all types indexing a basis of V . So instead it is defined as the supremum Module.rank K V of cardinals of all linearly independent sets in V . If V has universe level u then its rank has type Cardinal.{u} .
html: But we cannot directly compare cardinals in different universes. So technically we cannot define the rank of a vector space V as the cardinal of all types indexing a basis of V. So instead it is defined as the supremum Module.rank K V of cardinals of all linearly independent sets in V. If V has universe level u then its rank has type Cardinal.{u}.
zh: 但我们不能直接比较不同宇宙中的基数。因此，从技术上说，不能把向量空间 V 的秩定义为所有可作为 V 的基指标类型的基数。于是，秩改定义为 V 中所有线性无关集的基数的上确界 Module.rank K V。若 V 的宇宙层级为 u，则其秩的类型为 Cardinal.{u}。

text: One can still relate this definition to bases. Indeed there is also a commutative max operation on universe levels, and given two universe levels u and v there is an operation Cardinal.lift.{u, v} : Cardinal.{v} → Cardinal.{max v u} that allows to put cardinals in a common universe and state the dimension theorem.
html: One can still relate this definition to bases. Indeed there is also a commutative max operation on universe levels, and given two universe levels u and v there is an operation Cardinal.lift.{u, v} : Cardinal.{v} → Cardinal.{max v u} that allows to put cardinals in a common universe and state the dimension theorem.
zh: 仍然可以把这个定义与基联系起来。事实上，宇宙层级上还有一个交换的最大值运算 max；给定两个宇宙层级 u 和 v，有一个操作 Cardinal.lift.{u, v} : Cardinal.{v} → Cardinal.{max v u}，它允许把基数放入共同的宇宙中，从而陈述维数定理。

text: We can relate the finite dimensional case to this discussion using the coercion from natural numbers to finite cardinals (or more precisely the finite cardinals which live in Cardinal.{v} where v is the universe level of V ).
html: We can relate the finite dimensional case to this discussion using the coercion from natural numbers to finite cardinals (or more precisely the finite cardinals which live in Cardinal.{v} where v is the universe level of V).
zh: 可以通过从自然数到有限基数的强制转换，把有限维情形与上述讨论联系起来（更精确地说，是到生活在 Cardinal.{v} 中的有限基数，其中 v 是 V 的宇宙层级）。
